title: AI Operations Specialist
key_evidence: Requirements partial fit: wants 'demonstrated experience deploying AI tools (Claude, Cursor, n8n, Zapier) in actual business environments' + low-code/no-code, APIs, SQL/BI familiarity + 'builder's mentality' — Ryan's Claude Code agent-building and operator background hit the AI-builder and ownership parts.

HARD-ER GATE: '2-5 years in product operations, business operations, strategy, or technical program management.' Ryan's 6.4yr is marketing/community/founder-associate, not titled product/biz-ops or TPM — adjacent but not a clean keyword match. Also 'design and build multi-step agentic workflows that connect AI models with internal systems' leans more technical than Moss's framing.
Company: Capital on Tap is a ~971-employee, ~$227M-revenue established London fintech (business credit cards, founded 2012, 200k+ SMB customers). Large, mature, regulated-adjacent financial services.

SCREENING RISK: ~971 employees with a full talent/recruiting function on Greenhouse ATS — first gate is recruiter/keyword filter. A CV lacking the literal 'product/business operations / TPM' title and a regulated-fintech background is at real risk of being filtered.
Posting on Greenhouse; date not exposed. In-office 3 days/week London — reasonable cadence, salary not stated but established fintech likely clears £50k.

Domain = SME credit/fintech, regulated-adjacent — outside Ryan's crypto/AI-tooling home turf; the AI-enablement nature of the role makes domain ramp moderate, but he is not differentiated/memorable here.
biggest_lever: The role's central ask — a hands-on builder who deploys Claude/n8n/Zapier agentic workflows into real business operations — overlaps directly with Ryan's current Claude Code agent work, giving him a credible AI-builder story.
biggest_risk: Explicit '2-5 years in product/business operations/strategy/TPM' requirement plus a large-fintech recruiter/ATS gate; Ryan's marketing/community/founder-associate titles don't keyword-match the ops/TPM gate and the agentic-workflow build is more technical than his proven depth.
apply_verdict: Mid-priority — apply if Ryan can reframe his founder-associate/operator experience as business-operations and lead hard with concrete Claude-Code agentic-workflow builds; but the titled-ops gate + large-company ATS make it lower-odds than Conduct or Corgi.
recency_note: Greenhouse first_published 2026-04-23 (47d / ~6.7wk old), updated_at 2026-05-21 (refreshed ~18d ago but not a true repost). 4-8wk bracket (G=32) — freshest-but-one in batch. No applicant count via API. Callback 58 -> 52.
momentum_signals: STRONG: 51 open roles on Greenhouse (20 London) across 9 departments — broad active hiring at a mature ~971-person fintech (boards-api.greenhouse.io/capitalontap, 2026-06-09).

AI-TEAM BUILD-OUT (key): THREE concurrent AI roles — AI Operations Specialist (London) + Applied AI Specialist (London) both refreshed 2026-05-21, plus Senior Software Engineer (AI, Porto) posted TODAY 2026-06-09. This role is part of a genuine AI cohort, not a one-off backfill.
TOOLING VALIDATION: careers-page tech stack explicitly lists Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Gemini — confirms the JD's Claude/n8n agentic-workflow ask is real internal practice, not aspirational.

NO NEGATIVE SIGNALS: no Capital-on-Tap-specific layoff/restructure news found (broad 2026 fintech-layoff articles do NOT name them); macro London fintech hiring forecast +37% in 2026 (Morgan McKinley) is a tailwind.
Source: Greenhouse API + WebFetch careers + WebSearch, 2026-06-09.
operator_fit_note: PARTIAL - central ask is hands-on Claude/n8n agentic-workflow builder in real ops (strong spike hit), but gated on titled product/biz-ops/TPM, agentic builds lean technical, and it's a mature 971-person fintech (recruiter/ATS screen, not founder-facing).
